definitions.definition: A haploid reproductive cell, either male or female, that fuses with another gamete during fertilization to form a zygote. Male gametes are produced by the anther of a stamen, while female gametes are produced by the ovule of an ovary.
definitions.example: Sperm and egg cells are examples of gametes.

definitions.definition: A specialized type of cell division that reduces the number of chromosomes in a cell from diploid to haploid, resulting in the production of gametes.
definitions.example: Meiosis occurs in the reproductive organs of flowering plants.

definitions.definition: The fusion of a male gamete with a female gamete to form a zygote, which is the first step in the development of a new individual.
definitions.example: Fertilization occurs in the ovary of a flower.

definitions.definition: The cell that results from the fusion of two gametes during fertilization, which develops into a new individual through a series of cell divisions.
definitions.example: The zygote is formed when a sperm cell fertilizes an egg cell.

definitions.definition: Having two sets of chromosomes, one set inherited from each parent, which is characteristic of somatic cells in most organisms.
definitions.example: The cells of the roots and stems of a plant are diploid.

definitions.definition: Having one set of chromosomes, which is characteristic of gametes in most organisms.
definitions.example: The cells of the pollen and ovules of a flower are haploid.

definitions.definition: A part of the stamen that produces microspores, which develop into male gametophytes.
definitions.example: Microsporangia are found in the anthers of flowers.

definitions.definition: A small, haploid spore produced by the microsporangium that develops into a male gametophyte.
definitions.example: Microspores are produced in large numbers in the anthers of flowers.

definitions.definition: The haploid phase of the life cycle of a plant that produces gametes, typically found in the pollen of a flower.
definitions.example: The male gametophyte is formed from a microspore.

definitions.definition: A part of the ovary that produces megaspores, which develop into female gametophytes.
definitions.example: Megasporangia are found in the ovules of flowers.

definitions.definition: A large, haploid spore produced by the megasporangium that develops into a female gametophyte.
definitions.example: Megaspores are produced in smaller numbers than microspores.

definitions.definition: The haploid phase of the life cycle of a plant that produces gametes, typically found in the ovules of a flower.
definitions.example: The female gametophyte is formed from a megaspore.

definitions.definition: The process of fusion of two gametes to form a zygote.
A process in which two sperm cells fertilize two nuclei in the ovule, resulting in the formation of a zygote and an endosperm.
definitions.example: Double fertilization occurs in the ovary of a flower.

definitions.definition: A triploid tissue that provides nutrients to the developing embryo and seedling.
definitions.example: Endosperm is formed from one sperm cell and the polar nuclei of the ovule.

definitions.definition: The number of sets of chromosomes in an organism's cells.
definitions.example: Diploid and haploid cells have different ploidy levels.
